key_insight: A DVAE plus Particle Filter can monitor arc welding states robustly across different welding processes by learning compact latent dynamics and filtering noise in real time.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable path to generalizable weld monitoring that reduces reliance on large labeled datasets and process-specific tuning.
method: Unsupervised DVAE learns compact latent features from weld pool images.
CNN encoder-decoder captures visual structure; LSTM transition model learns time-evolving latent dynamics under control inputs.
Particle Filter propagates latent and hidden states to preserve history while suppressing arc radiation and reflection noise.
evidence_signal: The framework was validated on GTAW and GMAW and reported to work without process-specific tuning, supporting generalizability and robustness.
watch_item: Performance may depend on stable image quality and careful PF state design, since severe visual occlusion or large process shifts could challenge inference.
paper_title: Robust Monitoring of Arc Welding Processes: A Generalizable Framework with DVAE and Particle Filter
